body.title: CLOSED: SOAR-style impasse handler in coa.py
body.result: SOAR impasse handler in coa.py: _ne_stuck_count tracks consecutive no-result cycles; at count==3 pushes IMPASSE subgoal to TWM salience=0.9
body.description:
Igor's NE has been producing 'no result — nothing actionable in TWM' for 12+ consecutive hours (2026-05-17). There is no impasse detection — the system loops identically. SOAR-style architectures detect this state and create a subgoal to resolve it.

Add a consecutive-no-result counter to coa.py _ne_worker(). When NE produces no result for 3+ consecutive cycles, write a TWM entry: 'IMPASSE: NE produced no result {N} consecutive cycles — self-diagnostic subgoal' at salience=0.9 category='impasse'. This gives the next NE cycle actionable content: diagnose what's blocking cognition. Reset counter when NE produces a result.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia — Igor authors with discussion); theigors/rules/database (Postgres only, no sqlite)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: counter tracking + twm_push() call in _ne_worker(). Out of scope: NE response to the subgoal content (emergent behavior).
**Test plan:** After restart with Ollama mocked to return empty result, channel shows '[IMPASSE] self-diagnostic subgoal posted' after 3 no-result cycles. Subsequent cycles have actionable TWM content. No more infinite identical '[NE] cycle produced no result' loop without diagnostic content.
